sn22.87:1.1: At one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels' feeding ground.
sn22.87:1.11: ‘It would be *sādhu*, *bhante*, if the *Bhagavā* would go to Vakkali the *bhikkhu*, out of compassion.’”
sn22.87:1.2: Now at that time, Venerable Vakkali was dwelling in a potter's residence, sick, suffering, gravely ill.
sn22.87:1.3: Then Venerable Vakkali addressed his attendants:
sn22.87:1.4: “Come, friends, go to the *Bhagavā*; having gone, bow with your head at the *Bhagavā*'s feet in my name:
sn22.87:1.5: ‘*Bhante*, Vakkali the *bhikkhu* is sick, suffering, gravely ill; he bows with his head at the *Bhagavā*'s feet.’
sn22.87:1.7: ‘It would be *sādhu*, *bhante*, if the *Bhagavā* would go to Vakkali the *bhikkhu*, out of compassion.’”
sn22.87:1.8: “Yes, friend,” those *bhikkhus* replied to Venerable Vakkali. They went to the *Bhagavā*; having gone, they paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat to one side. Sitting to one side, those *bhikkhus* said to the *Bhagavā*:
sn22.87:1.9: “*Bhante*, Vakkali the *bhikkhu* is sick, suffering, gravely ill; he bows with his head at the *Bhagavā*'s feet;
sn22.87:10.2: “Come, *bhikkhus*, let us go to the Black Rock on the slope of Isigili,
sn22.87:10.3: where Vakkali the clansman used the knife on himself.”
sn22.87:10.5: Then the *Bhagavā*, together with several *bhikkhus*, went to the Black Rock on the slope of Isigili.
sn22.87:10.6: The *Bhagavā* saw Venerable Vakkali from afar, lying on the little bed with his shoulder turned.
sn22.87:11.1: Now at that time, a smokiness, a darkness, was moving to the eastern direction, moving to the western direction, moving to the northern direction, moving to the southern direction, moving upward, moving downward, moving to the intermediate directions.
sn22.87:11.3: “Do you see, *bhikkhus*, that smokiness, that darkness, moving to the eastern direction … moving to the intermediate directions?”
sn22.87:11.5: “That, *bhikkhus*, is Māra the Evil One, searching for the *viññāṇa* of Vakkali the clansman:
sn22.87:11.6: ‘Where is the *viññāṇa* of Vakkali the clansman established?’
sn22.87:11.7: But with *viññāṇa* unestablished, *bhikkhus*, Vakkali the clansman has attained final *nibbāna*.”
sn22.87:2.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having dressed and taken his bowl and robe, went to Venerable Vakkali.
sn22.87:2.10: “Indeed, *bhante*, I have much remorse, much regret.”
sn22.87:2.11: “But I hope, Vakkali, your self does not reproach you in regard to virtue?”
sn22.87:2.12: “No, *bhante*, my self does not reproach me in regard to virtue.”
sn22.87:2.13: “If, Vakkali, your self does not reproach you in regard to virtue, then what is your remorse, what is your regret?”
sn22.87:2.14: “For a long time, *bhante*, I have wanted to go to see the *Bhagavā*, but there is not enough strength in my body for me to go to see the *Bhagavā*.”
sn22.87:2.2: Venerable Vakkali saw the *Bhagavā* coming from afar. Seeing him, he stirred on the little bed.
sn22.87:2.3: Then the *Bhagavā* said to Venerable Vakkali:
sn22.87:2.4: “Enough, Vakkali, do not stir on the little bed.
sn22.87:2.5: These seats are prepared; I will sit there.”
sn22.87:2.6: The *Bhagavā* sat on the prepared seat. Sitting, the *Bhagavā* said to Venerable Vakkali:
sn22.87:2.7: “I hope you are bearing up, Vakkali; I hope you are getting by. I hope painful *vedanās* are subsiding, not increasing; their subsiding is evident, not their increasing?”
sn22.87:2.8: “I am not bearing up, *bhante*; I am not getting by. Strong painful *vedanās* are increasing in me, not subsiding; their increasing is evident, not their subsiding.”
sn22.87:2.9: “I hope, Vakkali, you have no remorse, no regret?”
sn22.87:3.1: “Enough, Vakkali. What use is seeing this foul body to you?
sn22.87:3.2: One who sees the *dhamma*, Vakkali, sees me;
sn22.87:3.3: one who sees me sees the *dhamma*.
sn22.87:3.4: For seeing the *dhamma*, Vakkali, one sees me;
sn22.87:4.18: he understands: ‘There is no more for this state of being.’”
sn22.87:4.4: “But what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], is that *dukkha* or *sukha*?”
sn22.87:4.6: “But what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, subject to change, is it fitting to regard it thus:
sn22.87:4.7: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’?”
sn22.87:5.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having instructed Venerable Vakkali with this instruction, rose from his seat and went to Vulture Peak Mountain.
sn22.87:5.11: “Surely, *bhante*, well liberated, he will be liberated.”
sn22.87:5.13: Having said this, they paid homage to the *Bhagavā*, circumambulated him keeping him on their right, and vanished right there.
sn22.87:5.2: Then, not long after the *Bhagavā* had gone, Venerable Vakkali addressed his attendants:
sn22.87:5.3: “Come, friends, lift me onto the little bed and go to the Black Rock on the slope of Isigili.
sn22.87:5.4: For how could one such as I think to make an end inside a house?”
sn22.87:5.5: “Yes, friend,” those *bhikkhus* replied to Venerable Vakkali. Having lifted Venerable Vakkali onto the little bed, they went to the Black Rock on the slope of Isigili.
sn22.87:5.6: Then the *Bhagavā* spent that night and the remainder of that day on Vulture Peak Mountain.
sn22.87:5.7: Then, as the night advanced, two *devatās* of surpassing beauty, illuminating the whole of Vulture Peak, went to the *Bhagavā* … and stood to one side.
sn22.87:5.8: Standing to one side, one *devatā* said to the *Bhagavā*:
sn22.87:5.9: “*Bhante*, Vakkali the *bhikkhu* is setting his mind on liberation.”
sn22.87:6.1: Then, when that night had passed, the *Bhagavā* addressed the *bhikkhus*:
sn22.87:6.2: “Come, *bhikkhus*, go to Vakkali the *bhikkhu*; having gone, say this to Vakkali the *bhikkhu*:
sn22.87:7.1: ‘Listen, friend Vakkali, to the *Bhagavā*'s word and to the words of two *devatās*.
sn22.87:7.10: Your death will not be bad; your passing away will not be bad.’”
sn22.87:7.11: “Yes, *bhante*,” those *bhikkhus* replied to the *Bhagavā*. They went to Venerable Vakkali; having gone, they said to Venerable Vakkali:
sn22.87:7.12: “Listen, friend Vakkali, to the *Bhagavā*'s word and to the words of two *devatās*.”
sn22.87:7.2: Friend, tonight, as the night advanced, two *devatās* of surpassing beauty, illuminating the whole of Vulture Peak, went to the *Bhagavā*; having gone, they paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and stood to one side.
sn22.87:7.3: Standing to one side, friend, one *devatā* said this to the *Bhagavā*—
sn22.87:7.4: *Bhante*, Vakkali the *bhikkhu* is setting his mind on liberation.
sn22.87:7.6: Surely, *bhante*, well liberated, he will be liberated.
sn22.87:7.7: And the *Bhagavā* says this to you, friend Vakkali—
sn22.87:8.1: Then Venerable Vakkali addressed his attendants:
sn22.87:8.10: And the *Bhagavā* says this to you, friend Vakkali:
sn22.87:8.13: Your death will not be bad; your passing away will not be bad.’”
sn22.87:8.14: “Then, friends, bow with your head at the *Bhagavā*'s feet in my name:
sn22.87:8.15: ‘*Bhante*, Vakkali the *bhikkhu* is sick, suffering, gravely ill.
sn22.87:8.16: He bows with his head at the *Bhagavā*'s feet.’
sn22.87:8.19: About that, *bhante*, I do not doubt.
sn22.87:8.2: “Come, friends, lower me from the little bed.
sn22.87:8.20: I do not hesitate that what is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:8.21: I do not hesitate that, where something is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, subject to change, there is no desire or lust or affection in me for it.
sn22.87:8.23: About that, *bhante*, I do not doubt.
sn22.87:8.24: I do not hesitate that what is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:8.25: I do not hesitate that, where something is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, subject to change, there is no desire or lust or affection in me for it.
sn22.87:8.28: About that, *bhante*, I do not doubt.
sn22.87:8.29: I do not hesitate that what is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:8.3: For how could one such as I think to listen to that *Bhagavā*'s message while sitting on a high seat?”
sn22.87:8.30: I do not hesitate that, where something is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, subject to change, there is no desire or lust or affection in me for it.
sn22.87:8.32: About that, *bhante*, I do not doubt.
sn22.87:8.33: I do not hesitate that what is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:8.34: I do not hesitate that, where something is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, subject to change, there is no desire or lust or affection in me for it.’”
sn22.87:8.35: “Yes, friend,” those *bhikkhus* replied to Venerable Vakkali, and they departed.
sn22.87:8.36: Then, not long after those *bhikkhus* had departed, Venerable Vakkali used the knife on himself.
sn22.87:8.4: “Yes, friend,” those *bhikkhus* replied to Venerable Vakkali, and they lowered Venerable Vakkali from the little bed.
sn22.87:8.5: “Friend, tonight, as the night advanced, two *devatās* … stood to one side.
sn22.87:8.6: Standing to one side, friend, one *devatā* said to the *Bhagavā*:
sn22.87:8.7: ‘*Bhante*, Vakkali the *bhikkhu* is setting his mind on liberation.’
sn22.87:8.9: ‘Surely, *bhante*, well liberated, he will be liberated.’
sn22.87:9.1: Then those *bhikkhus* went to the *Bhagavā*; having gone, they sat to one side. Sitting to one side, those *bhikkhus* said to the *Bhagavā*:
sn22.87:9.13: About that, *bhante*, I do not doubt.
sn22.87:9.14: I do not hesitate that what is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:9.15: I do not hesitate that, where something is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, subject to change, there is no desire or lust or affection in me for it.’”
sn22.87:9.2: “*Bhante*, Vakkali the *bhikkhu* is sick, suffering, gravely ill;
sn22.87:9.3: he bows with his head at the *Bhagavā*'s feet;
sn22.87:9.6: About that, *bhante*, I do not doubt.
sn22.87:9.7: I do not hesitate that what is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:9.8: I do not hesitate that, where something is impermanent [*yadaniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, subject to change, there is no desire or lust or affection in me for it.
